senses.definition: with one another to achieve an aim

examples.x: The team is working in harness to complete the project on time.

senses.definition: to cooperate, work together, or engage in a mutually beneficial interaction or partnership with someone
to fulfill one's share of responsibilities

examples.x: If we all do our part, we can make a difference in our community.
If we want to win the championship, we'll all need to do our bit and give it our best effort.
She's been doing her part by volunteering at the local food bank every week.
The team succeeded because everyone did their bit and worked together effectively.
The students did their part by presenting their research at the conference.

word_list.word: (more|greater|better) than the sum of its parts
word_list.pronunciation: mˈoːɹ ɡɹˈeɪɾɚ bˈɛɾɚ ðɐn ðə sˈʌm ʌv ɪts pˈɑːɹts

senses.definition: surpassing in terms of quality or effectiveness when working as a whole rather than individually

examples.x: If we work together effectively, our company can be greater than the sum of its parts and achieve great success.
The organization is better than the sum of its parts, with each department working together to achieve a common mission.
The project was successful because each team member contributed their unique skills, making it more than the sum of its parts.
The team's success is more than the sum of its parts, as each member brings unique skills and strengths to the table.

senses.definition: to complete one's responsibilities regarding a group task, project, work, etc.

examples.x: If we all pull our weight and work together, we can achieve great things.
If you want to be part of the team, you need to pull your weight and contribute to the project.
She's been pulling her weight by working late and taking on extra tasks to help the team meet the deadline.
The project was successful because each team member pulled their weight and contributed to the effort.

senses.definition: two individuals who managed to plan and get something done together

examples.x: Emily and her sister are partners in crime when it comes to teasing their little brother.
I used to be partners in crime with my best friend when we were in high school.
Jamie used to be my partner in crime whenever I wanted to steal something from my dad's room.
Sarah and Tom are partners in crime when it comes to pulling pranks on their coworkers.

senses.definition: used to describe a close and cooperative relationship between two people or parties, often with the goal of achieving a common objective

examples.x: The director and producer are hand and glove during the pre-production process.
The sales team and marketing team work hand and glove to create effective campaigns.
The singer and songwriter are hand and glove when it comes to creating music.

senses.definition: used for saying that everyone's assistance is needed for a situation, particularly a difficult one

examples.x: If there's a fire in the building, we need all hands on deck to evacuate everyone safely.
The coach called for all hands on deck during the final minutes of the game, hoping to turn the score around.
We have a big project due tomorrow, so we need all hands on deck to get it done.
When the restaurant gets busy, the manager calls for all hands on deck to help with serving and cleaning.
